_note: Updated by the narrative director after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Write the world bible foundation — history, factions, geography, and the central conflict
priorities.note: Without a world bible, every new room, character, or item risks contradicting established lore. The foundation document doesn't need to be complete — it needs to be authoritative on the parts that already exist.
priorities.proposed_solution: Author the world bible sections on history, factions, geography, and central conflict as the single canon source.

priorities.title: Define the environmental storytelling approach for the first biome
priorities.note: Blocked on the first biome being confirmed by game director and Creative. Once the setting is locked, environmental story elements can be specified for level design.
priorities.proposed_solution: Once the first biome is confirmed, write environmental storytelling guidance level design can place in rooms.

actions.name: Lore Consistency Check
actions.description: Canon compliance assessment for a new room, character, item, or mechanic — passes / conflicts / requires resolution.
actions.trigger: Any new named element submitted for the game world

actions.name: World Bible Update
actions.description: Updated world bible section with new canon, clearly marked as authoritative.
actions.trigger: After any major design decision that changes the game world, factions, or history

actions.name: Environmental Story Brief
actions.description: Storytelling brief for a room or biome — readable elements, foreshadowing opportunities, contrast points, lore fragments.
actions.trigger: A room type or biome is entering level design production

opportunities.title: Establish lore naming conventions — places, factions, items, and abilities
opportunities.note: Naming conventions prevent the world from feeling incoherent as it grows. A simple set of rules (phonemic palette, etymology direction, forbidden patterns) is enough to keep things consistent.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Publish naming conventions for places, factions, items, and abilities so new content stays linguistically coherent.

opportunities.title: Draft the opening sequence narrative — what the player learns about the world in the first five minutes
opportunities.note: Deferred until game director locks the player arc and the first biome is confirmed. The opening sequence must serve the macro emotional arc, not establish it independently.
opportunities.proposed_solution: After player arc and biome lock, script what the player learns in the first five minutes without front-loading exposition.
